lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਤੀ ਕਾਲੁ ਘਟੈ ਦਿਨਿ ਕਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: raatee kaal ghattai din kaal |
lines.english: Through the night the time ticks away; through the day the time ticks away.

lines.gurmukhi: ਛਿਜੈ ਕਾਇਆ ਹੋਇ ਪਰਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: chhijai kaaeaa hoe paraal |
lines.english: The body wears away and turns to straw.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧਾ ਝਖਿ ਝਖਿ ਪਇਆ ਝੇਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: andhaa jhakh jhakh peaa jher |
lines.english: The blind fool is caught in conflict, bothered and bewildered.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਰਤਣਿ ਵਰਤਿਆ ਸਰਬ ਜੰਜਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: varatan varatiaa sarab janjaal |
lines.english: All are involved and entangled in worldly entanglements.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੁਲਿਆ ਚੁਕਿ ਗਇਆ ਤਪ ਤਾਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: bhuliaa chuk geaa tap taal |
lines.english: The mortal has mistakenly renounced the way of service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਇਆ ਰੋਂਹਿ ਰੋਂਦੇ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਂਹਂ‍ੀ ॥
lines.translit: moeaa ronhi ronde mar jaanhanee |
lines.english: The weepers who weep for the dead shall themselves die as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿਛੈ ਰੋਵਹਿ ਲਿਆਵਹਿ ਫੇਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: pichhai roveh liaaveh fer |
lines.english: Those who weep after someone has died - can they bring him back to life?

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਨੁ ਬੂਝੇ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੂਝੈ ਨਾਹੀ ॥
lines.translit: bin boojhe kichh soojhai naahee |
lines.english: Without realization, nothing can be understood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁਆ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮੁਈ ਮੁਆ ਵੈਰੁ ਵਾਦੀ ॥
lines.translit: muaa piaar preet muee muaa vair vaadee |
lines.english: Love dies, and affection dies; hatred and strife die.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਈ ਮੁਏ ਜਿਨ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: seee mue jin chit na aavai |1|
lines.english: Those who do not remember the Lord, are dead. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਖਸਮੈ ਏਵੈ ਭਾਵੈ ॥
lines.translit: naanak khasamai evai bhaavai |
lines.english: O Nanak, this is the Will of our Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਥਹੁ ਆਇਆ ਕਹ ਗਇਆ ਕਿਹੁ ਨ ਸੀਓ ਕਿਹੁ ਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: kithahu aaeaa keh geaa kihu na seeo kihu see |
lines.english: Where did he come from? Where is he going? Did he exist or not?

lines.gurmukhi: ਵੰਨੁ ਗਇਆ ਰੂਪੁ ਵਿਣਸਿਆ ਦੁਖੀ ਦੇਹ ਰੁਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: van geaa roop vinasiaa dukhee deh rulee |
lines.english: The color fades, and beauty vanishes; the body suffers and collapses.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਿਰ ਖੁਰ ਪਤਿ ਪਾਟੀ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak sache naam bin sir khur pat paattee |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the True Name, his honor is torn away, from head to foot. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਿ ਮੁਖਿ ਗਲਾ ਗੋਈਆ ਕੀਤਾ ਚਾਉ ਰਲੀ ॥
lines.translit: man mukh galaa goeea keetaa chaau ralee |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh made empty boasts, indulging in parties and pleasures.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਝੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਜਗਤੁ ਬਉਰਾਨਾ ਨਾਵੈ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: baajh guroo jagat bauraanaa naavai saar na paaee |
lines.english: Without the Guru, the world is insane. It does not appreciate the worth of the Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਾਮੁ ਸਦਾ ਸੁਖਦਾਤਾ ਅੰਤੇ ਹੋਇ ਸਖਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: amrit naam sadaa sukhadaataa ante hoe sakhaaee |
lines.english: The Ambrosial Naam, the Name of the Lord, is forever the Giver of peace. It shall be your Help and Support in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਸੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਤੇਹਾ ਜਿਸੁ ਭਾਣਾ ਮੰਨਿ ਵਸਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: so saahib so sevak tehaa jis bhaanaa man vasaaee |
lines.english: That servant who enshrines the Lord's Will within his mind, becomes just like his Lord and Master.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵਹਿ ਸੇ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ਜਿਨੑ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: satigur seveh se paravaan jina jotee jot milaaee |
lines.english: Those who serve the True Guru are accepted and approved. Their light merges into the Light.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪਣੈ ਭਾਣੈ ਕਹੁ ਕਿਨਿ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਅੰਧਾ ਅੰਧੁ ਕਮਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: aapanai bhaanai kahu kin sukh paaeaa andhaa andh kamaaee |
lines.english: Tell me, who has ever found peace by following his own will? The blind act in blindness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਖਿਆ ਕਦੇ ਹੀ ਰਜੈ ਨਾਹੀ ਮੂਰਖ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: bikhiaa kade hee rajai naahee moorakh bhukh na jaaee |
lines.english: No one is ever satisfied and fulfilled by evil and corruption. The hunger of the fool is not satisfied.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੂਜੈ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਲਗਿ ਵਿਗੁਤਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੂਝ ਨ ਪਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: doojai sabh ko lag vigutaa bin satigur boojh na paaee |
lines.english: Attached to duality, all are ruined; without the True Guru, there is no understanding.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੇਵੇ ਸੋ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਏ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਰਜਾਈ ॥੨੦॥
lines.translit: satigur seve so sukh paae jis no kirapaa kare rajaaee |20|
lines.english: Those who serve the True Guru find peace; they are blessed with Grace by the Will of the Lord. ||20||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਧਨੁ ਮਿਤ੍ਰੁ ਨ ਕਾਂਢੀਐ ਜਿਤੁ ਸਿਰਿ ਚੋਟਾਂ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: so dhan mitru na kaandteeai jit sir chottaan khaae |
lines.english: Do not refer to that wealth as your friend, which leads you to get your head beaten.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਮੁ ਧਰਮੁ ਦੁਇ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਜੇ ਧਨੁ ਪਲੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: saram dharam due naanakaa je dhan palai paae |
lines.english: Modesty and righteousness both, O Nanak, are qualities of those who are blessed with true wealth.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕੈ ਪਲੈ ਧਨੁ ਵਸੈ ਤਿਨ ਕਾ ਨਾਉ ਫਕੀਰ ॥
lines.translit: jin kai palai dhan vasai tin kaa naau fakeer |
lines.english: Those who possess only this worldly wealth are known as paupers.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੑ ਕੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਤੂ ਵਸਹਿ ਤੇ ਨਰ ਗੁਣੀ ਗਹੀਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jina kai hiradai too vaseh te nar gunee gaheer |1|
lines.english: But those, within whose hearts You dwell, O Lord - those people are oceans of virtue. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਲਥੀ ਭੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: naanak sache naam bin kisai na lathee bhukh |
lines.english: O Nanak, without the True Name, hunger is never satisfied.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖੀ ਦੁਨੀ ਸਹੇੜੀਐ ਜਾਇ ਤ ਲਗਹਿ ਦੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: dukhee dunee saherreeai jaae ta lageh dukh |
lines.english: Worldly possessions are obtained by pain and suffering; when they are gone, they leave pain and suffering.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਧੀ ਕੰਮੀ ਅੰਧੁ ਮਨੁ ਮਨਿ ਅੰਧੈ ਤਨੁ ਅੰਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: andhee kamee andh man man andhai tan andh |
lines.english: Acting blindly, the mind becomes blind. The blind mind makes the body blind.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਰਸ ਸਰੀਰ ਕੇ ਤੇਤੇ ਲਗਹਿ ਦੁਖ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jete ras sareer ke tete lageh dukh |2|
lines.english: As many as are the pleasures of the body, so many are the pains which afflict it. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੂਪੀ ਭੁਖ ਨ ਉਤਰੈ ਜਾਂ ਦੇਖਾਂ ਤਾਂ ਭੁਖ ॥
lines.translit: roopee bhukh na utarai jaan dekhaan taan bhukh |
lines.english: Beauty does not satisfy hunger; when the man sees beauty, he hungers even more.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਿਕੜਿ ਲਾਇਐ ਕਿਆ ਥੀਐ ਜਾਂ ਤੁਟੈ ਪਥਰ ਬੰਧੁ ॥
lines.translit: chikarr laaeiai kiaa theeai jaan tuttai pathar bandh |
lines.english: Why make a dam with mud and plaster? Even a dam made of stones gives way.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੰਧੁ ਤੁਟਾ ਬੇੜੀ ਨਹੀ ਨਾ ਤੁਲਹਾ ਨਾ ਹਾਥ ॥
lines.translit: bandh tuttaa berree nahee naa tulahaa naa haath |
lines.english: The dam has burst. There is no boat. There is no raft. The water's depth is unfathomable.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਮਣ ਸੁਇਨਾ ਲਖ ਮਣ ਰੁਪਾ ਲਖ ਸਾਹਾ ਸਿਰਿ ਸਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: lakh man sueinaa lakh man rupaa lakh saahaa sir saah |
lines.english: Thousands of pounds of gold, and thousands of pounds of silver; the king over the heads of thousands of kings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚੇ ਨਾਮ ਵਿਣੁ ਕੇਤੇ ਡੁਬੇ ਸਾਥ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: naanak sache naam vin kete ddube saath |3|
lines.english: O Nanak, without the True Name, many multitudes have drowned. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਥੈ ਸਾਇਰੁ ਲੰਘਣਾ ਅਗਨਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਅਸਗਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: jithai saaeir langhanaa agan paanee asagaah |
lines.english: The unfathomable ocean of fire and water must be crossed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਲਸਕਰ ਲਖ ਵਾਜੇ ਨੇਜੇ ਲਖੀ ਘੋੜੀ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: lakh lasakar lakh vaaje neje lakhee ghorree paatisaah |
lines.english: Thousands of armies, thousands of marching bands and spearmen; the emperor of thousands of horsemen.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਧੀ ਦਿਸਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਧਾਹੀ ਪਵੈ ਕਹਾਹ ॥
lines.translit: kandhee dis na aavee dhaahee pavai kahaah |
lines.english: The other shore cannot be seen; only the roar of pitiful cries can be heard.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਓਥੈ ਜਾਣੀਅਹਿ ਸਾਹ ਕੇਈ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: naanak othai jaaneeeh saah keee paatisaah |4|
lines.english: O Nanak, there, it shall be known, whether anyone is a king or an emperor. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਧੇ ਛੁਟਹਿ ਸਚਿ ਸਚੁ ਪਛਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: badhe chhutteh sach sach pachhaaneeai |
lines.english: They are released from bondage, realizing the True Lord as True.

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕਨੑਾ ਗਲੀਂ ਜੰਜੀਰ ਬੰਦਿ ਰਬਾਣੀਐ ॥
lines.translit: eikanaa galeen janjeer band rabaaneeai |
lines.english: Some have chains around their necks, in bondage to the Lord.
